name: agent-evals
description: Use to AUDIT an AI product's or workspace's integrations, score their feedback-loop readiness on the AI Optimization Staircase, and scaffold the smallest useful evaluation and optimization loop plus post-readiness improvement controllers. For teams shipping an AI product or agent who still iterate on prompts by feel and want metric-driven reliability. Covers eval suites, prompt and program optimizers, trace-and-eval flywheels, held-out benchmarks, and production experiments. Triggers on 'audit our AI integration', 'build an eval or optimization loop', 'are our prompts actually measured', 'map us onto the optimization staircase', 'set up a trace-and-eval flywheel'. Do NOT use to harden a repository for coding agents (use agent-readiness) or to promote observed agent failures into AGENTS.md rules from an existing signal (use agent-rules) — agent-evals instruments the AI product you ship, those harden the repo agents work in.

inspired_by.name: DSPy: Compiling Declarative Language Model Calls into Self-Improving Pipelines
inspired_by.author: Omar Khattab
inspired_by.contribution: Declarative LM framework: typed Signatures, Modules, and Optimizers (BootstrapFewShot, MIPROv2). Reframes prompt engineering as program design and gives Level 2 (Subroutine Compilation) its compilation target.

inspired_by.name: DSPy: The End of Prompt Engineering
inspired_by.author: Kevin Madura
inspired_by.contribution: Practical framing of declarative LM compilation as cognitive-subroutine engineering inside production AI workspaces. Anchors the skill's Level 2 scaffold recommendation.

inspired_by.name: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
inspired_by.author: Noah Shinn
inspired_by.contribution: Iteration caps, structured failure critique, and reflective self-improvement loops. Underwrites Level 1 (System-Prompt Learning) and the iteration-cap guardrails in Level 3.

inspired_by.name: Feedback-loop product engineering and eval/observability flywheels
inspired_by.author: Granola, Braintrust, Arize, Raindrop, Microsoft Foundry
inspired_by.contribution: Adds loop-readiness dimensions: signal capture, interpretation layer, change surface, cadence, stop/rollback rules, and human or operational ownership.

inspired_by.name: Self-improving AI systems and system prompt learning
inspired_by.author: AI Wiki
inspired_by.contribution: Adds the post-readiness distinction between measured loops, proposed updates, eval-gated persistence, and production experiments.

inspired_by.name: Terminal-Bench and SWE-bench style system benchmarks
inspired_by.author: Laude Institute and SWE-bench maintainers
inspired_by.contribution: Grounds Level 4 as fixed task replay with baseline comparison for model swaps, release gates, and platform-wide regression protection.

metadata.catalog_summary: audit an AI product's feedback loops, map them onto the AI Optimization Staircase, score the missing loop mechanics, and scaffold the smallest useful eval/optimization loop. Diagnostic-first; never auto-writes learned rules without held-out evals plus a reviewed diff. Provenance in [`skills/agent-evals/skill.json`](./skills/agent-evals/skill.json).
